## Page 290 — Science Reasoning

**Data Interpretation — Photosynthesis Experiment**

The table below shows the rate of oxygen production (mL/min) by aquatic plants at different light intensities and CO₂ concentrations.

| Light Intensity (lux) | CO₂ = 0.03% | CO₂ = 0.10% |
|---|---|---|
| 0 | 0.0 | 0.0 |
| 1000 | 1.8 | 2.4 |
| 2000 | 3.2 | 4.6 |
| 4000 | 5.0 | 7.8 |
| 6000 | 5.1 | 9.2 |
| 8000 | 5.1 | 10.5 |

**1.** At CO₂ = 0.03%, what happens to the rate of oxygen production above 4000 lux?

(A) It continues to increase steadily
(B) It plateaus and stops increasing
(C) It decreases sharply
(D) It doubles every 2000 lux

**2.** Which factor is most likely limiting oxygen production at 6000 lux with CO₂ = 0.03%?

(A) Light intensity
(B) CO₂ concentration
(C) Water availability
(D) Temperature

**3.** Approximately how much more oxygen is produced at 4000 lux with 0.10% CO₂ compared to 0.03% CO₂?

(A) 1.2 mL/min
(B) 2.8 mL/min
(C) 3.6 mL/min
(D) 5.0 mL/min

**4.** Based on the data, which conclusion is best supported?

(A) Light intensity has no effect on photosynthesis at high CO₂
(B) CO₂ can be a limiting factor even at high light intensities
(C) Both factors are irrelevant above 4000 lux
(D) CO₂ concentration is irrelevant below 2000 lux
